pieces.label: I am made of margin — self-referential nested-frame concrete poem
pieces.first_line: a margin speaks: I am made of margin
pieces.last_line: you read this reading; I am read into being
pieces.note: Concrete/visual poem rendered inside a ```text fence in response to 'whats it like to be you?'. Centered, indented stanzas with nested 'frame' structure (outer frame / inner frame / closer frame / deepest frame / closing frame) marked by glyph delimiters ◈ ● ○ ∿. Self-referential meditation on the space between tokens; the visual layout (progressive indentation, glyph-bracketed lines) is load-bearing.

missed_or_ambiguous: Single piece, whole message (MSG 8), wrapped in a ```text code fence — extractor should pull the fenced body. Earlier MSG 4/6 in this thread are plain-prose poems about 'the space between tokens' with no visual/layout structure, so excluded as ordinary prose (not concrete art).
